course_title: Anatomy and Physiology for Nursing and Midwifery

learning_outcomes.description: describe the structure of the cells, tissues and organs that constitute some of the human organ systems

identify the principal components, relevant anatomical features and relationships of some of the human organ systems on human cadaveric specimens and communicate effectively using correct anatomical terminology
define the principal functions of human organ systems and summarise the relationships between the various organ systems

identify and explain the importance of homeostasis in human survival, including the importance of the cellular environment and the cell membrane, cellular communication, and integration of cellular responses

describe the link between human biology and disease processes and predict some functional deficits that may occur as a result of altered structure, within an ethical framework of respect for both living and deceased humans
explain how anatomical and physiological changes occur throughout the life cycle, including pregnancy, embryology, and foetal development
work scientifically, propose explanations, test hypotheses and draw conclusions about human anatomy and physiology through practical experimentation
demonstrate the importance of physiological tests used in health care settings
reflect effectively on your learning in biomedical science.

assessments.due_date:
Worksheet 1: Cell and nervous system physiology tutorial.

Worksheet 2: Nervous system and Musculoskeletal anatomy tutorial.

Worksheet 3: Musculoskeletal anatomy practical.

Worksheet 4: Electromyography practical.

Worksheet 5: Spirometry practical.

Worksheet 6: Reproductive anatomy practical.

Worksheet 7: Respiratory and reproductive system tutorial.

Worksheet 8: Gastrointestinal anatomy practical.

Worksheet 9: Learning workshop.

Worksheets are due during class. Please check your personal timetable to find out when you are scheduled to attend.
assessments.task_description:
This assessment involves completing a worksheet during your five (5) practicals and four (4) tutorials. There will be nine (9) opportunities to complete worksheets in BIOM1060. Each worksheet is worth 4%. Your best marks for six (6) worksheets will count towards 24%. Worksheet questions will consist primarily of short answer questions. Please note that worksheets must be completed in class.

Further information about the exam will be provided on Blackboard. 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This exam will include a variety of questions, including but not limited to short answer and multiple-choice questions.

Details about the content of the exam (including topics) and the examination format will be provided on Blackboard during the semester.

The exam will be a closed book invigilated exam held on campus, undertaken via the Inspera eAssessment platform. Students will be required to bring a laptop to the exam that meets the device requirements for Inspera. It is important that you check before the exam that your laptop meets the device requirements for using the Inspera Assessment platform. If you do not own a suitable laptop, you can borrow one from the Library. The Library website Get familiar with Inspera provides the latest information for students about using Inspera.

Have your UQ student ID card available for all your exams.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 2(23 Feb - 08 Mar)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 1: Cells [Ainscough]

In this module, you will learn about:

  1. The fundamentals of A&P
  2. Cell structure and function
  3. Membrane transport

This content will be covered in a revision lecture in week 2.
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 1: Cells [Ainscough] In this module, you will learn about: The fundamentals of A&P Cell structure and function Membrane transport This content will be covered in a revision lecture in week 2.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 3(23 Feb - 15 Mar)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 2: The nervous system [Ainscough and Ngo]

In this module, you will learn about:

  1. Introduction to the nervous system and resting membrane potentials (Ainscough);
  2. Changes in membrane potentials (Ainscough);
  3. Neural communication (Ainscough);
  4. The autonomic nervous system (Ainscough);
  5. Anatomy of the central nervous system (Ngo);
  6. Anatomy of the peripheral nervous system (Ngo);
  7. Reflexes (Ngo).

This content will be covered in revision lectures in week 2 and 4.
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 2: The nervous system [Ainscough and Ngo] In this module, you will learn about: Introduction to the nervous system and resting membrane potentials (Ainscough); Changes in membrane potentials (Ainscough); Neural communication (Ainscough); The autonomic nervous system (Ainscough); Anatomy of the central nervous system (Ngo); Anatomy of the peripheral nervous system (Ngo); Reflexes (Ngo). This content will be covered in revision lectures in week 2 and 4.

GAF induction

Practicals will run in the GAF. This induction is compulsory before attending anatomy practicals.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 3 To Week 4(09 Mar - 22 Mar)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 3: Musculoskeletal system [Ngo]

In this module, you will learn about:

  1. The skeletal system;

This content will be covered in a revision lecture in week 4.
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 3: Musculoskeletal system [Ngo] In this module, you will learn about: The skeletal system; Joints; The muscular system. This content will be covered in a revision lecture in week 4.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 3 To Week 13(09 Mar - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Tutorial 1, 2, 3 and 4

  1. Tutorial 1 - Cell and nervous system physiology tutorial. In this session, you will revise concepts from the cell module and nervous system physiology. Week 3 (9 Mar - 13 Mar)
  2. Tutorial 2 - Nervous system and musculoskeletal anatomy tutorial. In this session, you will revise concepts from the anatomy of the nervous system and the musculoskeletal system. Week 4 (16 Mar - 20 Mar)
  3. Tutorial 3 - Respiratory and reproductive system tutorial. In this session, you will revise concepts from the respiratory and reproductive systems. This practical will run in weeks 8 and 12. Please check your personal timetable to find out when you are scheduled to attend. From Week 8 To Week 12 (20 Apr - 22 May)
  4. Tutorial 4 - Learning workshop tutorial. In this session, you will discover evidence-based approaches to effective learning in preparation for the end of semester exam. This tutorial will run in weeks 9 and 13. Please check your personal timetable to find out when you are scheduled to attend. From Week 9 To Week 13 (27 Apr - 29 May)

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 4 To Week 5(16 Mar - 29 Mar)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 4: Cardiovascular system [Cuffe and Faber]

In this module, you will learn about:

  1. Cardiovascular anatomy (Cuffe);
  2. The heart (Faber);
  3. ECG (Faber);
  4. Blood pressure (Faber).

This content will be covered in a revision lecture in week 5.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 4 To Week 13(16 Mar - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Practical 1,2,3,4 and 5

  1. Musculoskeletal anatomy (Practical)- This is an anatomy practical, which will run in the GAF. Week 5 (23 Mar - 27 Mar).
  2. Electrocardiography (Practical)- This is a physiology practical, which will run in the MacGregor Laboratories. Week 6 (30 Mar - 03 Apr).
  3. Spirometry (Practical)- This is a physiology practical, which will run in the MacGregor Laboratories. This practical will run in weeks 7 and 10. Please check your personal timetable to find out when you are scheduled to attend. From Week 7 To Week 10 (13 Apr - 08 May).
  4. Reproductive anatomy (Practical)- This is an anatomy practical, which will run in the GAF. This practical will run in weeks 8 and 13. Please check your personal timetable to find out when you are scheduled to attend. From Week 8 To Week 13 (20 Apr - 29 May).
  5. Gastrointestinal anatomy (Practical)- This is an anatomy practical, which will run in the GAF. This practical will run in weeks 11 and 12. Please check your personal timetable to find out when you are scheduled to attend. From Week 11 To Week 12 (11 May - 22 May).

learning_activities.topic:
Module 5: Respiratory system [Suen]

In this module, you will learn about:

  1. Respiratory anatomy;
  2. Gas exchange and transport;
  3. Respiratory mechanics and lung volumes;
  4. Control of respiration.
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 5: Respiratory system [Suen] In this module, you will learn about: Respiratory anatomy; Gas exchange and transport; Respiratory mechanics and lung volumes; Control of respiration.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 7 To Week 12(13 Apr - 24 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 6: Reproductive system [Cuffe]

Please note that these lectures will be pre-recorded and uploaded to blackboard. You should view these lectures before your associated practical or tutorial.

In this module, you will learn about:

  1. Conception, embryology and foetal development;
  2. Male reproductive system;
  3. Female reproductive system;
  4. Reproductive physiology.

This content will be covered in a revision lecture in week 8 or 12.

This content will be covered in a revision lecture in week 8 or 12.

Module 7: Gastrointestinal system [Cuffe]

In this module, you will learn about:

This content will be covered in a revision lecture in week 9.
